name: faer
verdict: machine-precision on 9 closed-form checks; default features pull rayon->atomic-wait which breaks wasm32, use default-features=false + [std,linalg]

name: trussx
kind: 3D pin-jointed truss FEA (axial only, no bending)
verdict: member forces match method-of-joints to machine precision; tension-positive; only 2 stars but math is correct

name: frame-on-faer
verdict: our own ~80-line solver; cantilever/column/2-elem beam all machine-precision vs textbook; proves the building-frame path is buildable on faer

name: gemlab
kind: FEM toolkit: shapes, Gauss integ, mesh, stress recovery, CST stiffness (NOT a solver)
verdict: disk/ring moment integrals + elastic patch test machine-precision; needs C libs (suite-sparse/openblas/lapack) -> Linux container; continuum, not frames

name: pmsim
kind: full nonlinear solid/porous FEM solver (built on gemlab)
verdict: ships analytical validators (Lamé cylinder, Bhatti); will NOT build on arm64 mac (Intel MKL x86-only + MUMPS + version-skewed path deps); Linux-x86 container only

name: structural-shapes
kind: cross-section properties (I, A, moments) for steel shapes
verdict: helper not a solver; useful alongside a frame tool; not yet probed

name: finite_element_method
verdict: author-declared learning crate (2 stars); no load cases, no distributed loads, no validation; 3D-beam-with-bending unconfirmed

name: fe-engine
kind: beam+2D frame, load cases (dead/live/wind/seismic), steel+concrete code material libs
verdict: most building-shaped on paper but 2 stars, no releases, not on crates.io, GPU solver 'not validated', no real tests; closest skeleton if hardening one

name: quick-fea
verdict: 1 star MVP, distributed loads still 'stretch', not published; early/possibly-abandoned

name: oxiphysics
kind: claims to replace Bullet+OpenFOAM+LAMMPS+CalculiX in pure Rust
verdict: rigged validation gates (FEM 57% off -> PASS, MD energy 0->21.7 -> 'conserved'); 60k tests = near-zero signal; do not use
